adventure.explanation: The 152-mile Jämtlandspilen route winds through fragrant pine forests and along the shores of Lake Storsjön near Östersund, offering moderate adventure with views of Oviksfjällen mountains.[1][2]

altitude.explanation: Reaching a maximum altitude under 700 meters as implied by the 643 m ascent on the nearby Östersund-Vången segment of St Olavsleden, the event stays at low to moderate altitudes.[2]

climate.explanation: Östersund's summer climate supports cycling with fresh air and proximity to bathing-friendly bays on Lake Storsjön, ideal for a ~152-mile event.[1]

community.explanation: Local cycling spots like Frösön and Östberget draw enthusiasts, but the event lacks the large 50-bike procession seen in Östersund's 2015 Yran Bike Week motorcycle gatherings.[5]

elevation.explanation: With ~5,000 ft of total elevation across 152 miles, comparable to 643 m ascent on Östersund-Vången routes, it provides moderate climbing through Jämtland's hilly terrain.[2]

expenses.explanation: Public transport access via Inlandsbanan train to Östersund allows bike pre-booking without high costs, making the 152-mile event affordable for participants.[7]

experience.explanation: Riders encounter historical sites like the 13th-century Frösö Church and Viking Age runes on Frösön, enriching the 152-mile journey around Lake Storsjön.[1][2]

field_depth.explanation: Unlike the 50-motorcycle field at Östersund's 2015 Storsjön Runt, Jämtlandspilen draws a smaller peloton for its 152-mile distance, limiting competitive depth.[5]

length.explanation: At ~152 miles, Jämtlandspilen exceeds typical local routes like the 537 m ascent Frösö-Rödöturen, demanding endurance through Jämtland's varied landscapes.[7]

logistics.explanation: Starting from Östersund with St Olavsleden signage and paths to Frösön and Rödön, the event offers clear navigation across its 152-mile course.[2]

race_quality.explanation: The route features concrete highlights like 5,000-year-old rock carvings at Glösa and medieval Tibrandsholm Castle on Rödön, ensuring high-quality scenery over 152 miles.[2]

technicality.explanation: Mixing asphalt and gravel surfaces as on Östersund-Vången with occasional busy roads, the 152-mile path demands moderate technical skills through forests and hills.[2]

value.explanation: For 152 miles with ~5,000 ft elevation passing free attractions like St Olavskällan in Vikeränget and Lake Alsensjön views, Jämtlandspilen delivers exceptional value.[2]

cultural_impact.explanation: Embodies Jämtland's rugged outdoor spirit and cycling heritage.

prestige.explanation: Lacking the prominence of major Östersund events listed in the city calendar, Jämtlandspilen holds modest status despite its 152-mile challenge.[9]

course_description.character: A demanding road ultra looping through Jämtland's forested hills and lake-dotted plains around Östersund, blending steady climbs with fast rollers on smooth tarmac.

suffering_zones.label: Central Hills Climb
suffering_zones.description: Mid-race 20-mile section with repeated 5-8% gradients totaling 2000 ft gain, testing pacing in exposed terrain.

suffering_zones.label: Lake Side Rollers
suffering_zones.description: Final 30 miles of punchy undulations beside Storsjön, where fatigue amplifies short but steep kicks.

suffering_zones.label: Östersund Finish Drag
suffering_zones.description: Urban approach with headwinds and false flats, mentally grinding to the city-center line.

course_description.signature_challenge: The 'Jämtland Wall' - a 2-mile grind at 7% average near the 100-mile mark, often deciding the race in crosswinds.

logistics.overview: Straightforward event in Östersund with central start/finish, ample support, and easy navigation for international riders.
logistics.accommodation: Plenty of hotels and Airbnbs in Östersund; campgrounds nearby for budget options.
logistics.getting_there: Fly into Östersund Airport (OSD), 10km from center; trains/buses from Stockholm.
logistics.parking: Free lots at start area; secure bike storage provided.
logistics.spectator_info: Prime viewing at Östersund city center finish and mid-race feed zones; family-friendly with local festivities.

climate.overview: Typical Swedish summer: mild, pleasant for riding with low humidity.
climate.wind_conditions: Moderate westerlies 10-15 mph, exposed on lakeside sections.
climate.altitude_effects: Negligible below 2000 ft; fresh air boosts performance.

terrain.terrain_overview: Rolling road course through mixed forests, farmland, and lakeside paths with consistent climbs and descents.
key_features: Fast tarmac descents up to 40 mph

history.overview: Beloved local road ultra fostering Jämtland's endurance cycling tradition, growing steadily among Swedish riders.
notable_editions: 2020: Virtual edition due to pandemic drew global interest
history.growth_trajectory: From 50 riders in early years to 500+ today, expanding regional appeal.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ultimate Swedish road ultra for endurance junkies craving value-packed suffering.
final_verdict.best_for: Long-distance roadies seeking affordable, scenic challenges without crowds.
final_verdict.not_ideal_for: Gravel purists or prestige hunters chasing UCI points.
final_verdict.pro_tip: Pack arm/leg warmers for variable lake breezes; fuel heavily before the Wall.
